name: Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop
name_original: Le long de l'étang de Lacanau
description: An easy 4.8-mile (7.7 km) hike around Lacanau Lake, offering tranquil views, pine forests, and wildlife spotting opportunities.
region_intro: Hiking around Lacanau offers diverse terrain, blending ocean, lake, and forest environments. The region features the large freshwater Lacanau Lake with its calm waters and wooded shores, alongside extensive pine forests and coastal dunes. These varied landscapes provide a range of experiences for different hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Lacanau The most popular hiking route is Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This easy route offers tranquil views along the lake's wooded shores. Another top favourite among local hikers is Longarisse Forest and Lake Loop, an easy 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. It leads through shaded pine forests with views of Lacanau Lake. Local hikers also love the Loop in the Etang du Cousseau nature reserve, a 6.4 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through the unique swamp landscape of the Étang de Cousseau Nature Reserve, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Lacanau is defined by serene lake views, extensive pine forests, and coastal dunes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging paths. The routes in Lacanau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lacanau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Lacanau?
A: Lacanau offers a diverse range of terrain, blending ocean, lake, and extensive pine forest environments. You'll find tranquil paths along the wooded shores of Lacanau Lake, shaded routes through vast pine forests, and unique swamp landscapes within the Étang de Cousseau Nature Reserve. Coastal dunes also provide varied walking surfaces, ensuring a different experience for every hike.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in Lacanau?
A: Yes, Lacanau boasts a wide selection of hiking trails. There are over 100 routes available, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. This includes more than 60 easy trails, over 40 moderate options, and a dozen more challenging routes for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes around Lacanau Lake?
A: Absolutely. The area around Lacanau Lake is perfect for easy, family-friendly walks. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e840988414/longarisse-lacanau-lake-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer tranquil views along the wooded shores, ideal for a relaxed outing. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e833528114/longarisse-forest-and-lake-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Longarisse Forest and Lake Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines lake views with shaded forest paths.

Q: Can I find circular walking routes in Lacanau?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Lacanau are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e840988414/longarisse-lacanau-lake-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e833538916/loop-in-the-etang-du-cousseau-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop in the Etang du Cousseau nature reserve&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploration.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Lacanau?
A: Lacanau's trails offer access to several notable natural features. You can explore the unique ecosystem of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cousseau Pond Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, known for its swamp landscape and diverse wildlife. Hikers can also enjoy the serene views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;viewpoints of the pine forest&lt;/a&gt;, or walk along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4620641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway Between Lake Lacanau and Lake Hourtin&lt;/a&gt;. The various beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/965820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moutchic Beach&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic spots.

Q: Are there any trails that go through the Étang de Cousseau Nature Reserve?
A: Yes, the Étang de Cousseau Nature Reserve is a fantastic area for hiking. A popular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e833538916/loop-in-the-etang-du-cousseau-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop in the Etang du Cousseau nature reserve&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through its unique swamp landscape, offering opportunities for wildlife observation. There's also an &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/996619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Observation point – Free parking loop from Réserve naturelle de l'Étang du Cousseau&lt;/a&gt; for an easier exploration.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Lacanau?
A: The trails in Lacanau are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the peaceful lake shores to the shaded pine forests, and the well-maintained paths that make exploration enjoyable for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes in the Lacanau area?
A: While Lacanau is known for its easy and moderate trails, there are also more challenging options for those seeking a longer trek. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41482636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Free parking – Among the pines loop from Parking Marmande&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you through the extensive pine forests, offering a more extended experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lacanau?
A: Lacanau is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal for exploring the forests and lake shores. Summer is also popular, especially for combining hikes with beach visits, though trails can be busier. Winters are generally mild, allowing for peaceful walks, particularly in the pine forests.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the trails?
A: Yes, particularly within the Étang de Cousseau Nature Reserve. This ecological jewel is a resting place for migratory birds and features a diverse swamp landscape where you might spot various bird species and even individual cows grazing. The wooded areas around Lacanau Lake also offer chances to observe local wildlife.

Q: Can I find trails that lead through the pine forests?
A: Absolutely. Lacanau is nestled within an immense pine forest, offering numerous shaded and peaceful walking routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e833528114/longarisse-forest-and-lake-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Longarisse Forest and Lake Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading you directly through these serene woodlands with glimpses of the lake. Many other trails also weave through the extensive forest landscape.
tips: Along the Lacanau pond, the vegetation suddenly opens up to make way for a beautiful little pier. The boats there add even more charm to this beautiful place.
On the headland there are many small sandy beaches and shallow water. Well suited for swimming even for small children.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Longarisse, which serves as a significant starting point for exploring the lake's southwestern shore. You can typically find parking available in or near the Longarisse area, as it's a common access point for the lake.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop is generally considered an easy hike, suitable for all skill levels. It features easily accessible paths, making it a good choice for beginners and families with children. The total distance is approximately 7.7 km with minimal elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, offering a mix of natural elements. You'll walk along paths winding between reeds and water lilies at the lake's edge. Higher ground features wooded dunes and maritime pine forests. Sections may be paved, unpaved, gravel, or natural footpaths. If the loop extends towards areas like Maubuisson, you might encounter greenways crossing dune areas with ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy picturesque views of Lacanau Lake. Notable highlights include a Wooden pier on Lake Lacanau and the Lacanau Pond Viewpoint. The trail also offers shaded pine forests, beautiful beaches, isolated bays, and excellent viewpoints from elevated wooded dunes. The wilder southern parts are great for wildlife observation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, with the added benefit of fewer crowds. Summer is also a great time, especially if you plan to take a refreshing swimming break in the lake. The pine forests provide welcome shade during warmer months.

faqs.question: Is the Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, trails in natural areas like this are generally dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Longarisse - Lacanau Lake Loop. It's freely accessible for hikers to enjoy the natural beauty of the area.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the SLR Longarisse - Bayonne and the SLR Soulac-sur-Mer - Longarisse. The GR 8 long-distance hiking trail also skirts the lake, offering connections to other areas through the forest.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area, particularly the wilder southern parts of the lake accessible from Longarisse, is noted for wildlife observation. Being close to the Etang du Cousseau nature reserve, you might encounter diverse flora and fauna in a preserved environment. Keep an eye out for various bird species and other small animals typical of wetland and forest ecosystems.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or cafes near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural setting, Longarisse is a starting point for exploring the lake. You may find amenities like cafes or small shops in the nearby villages or towns around Lacanau Lake, such as Lacanau-Océan, which are accessible by car or potentially public transport.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this easy loop, comfortable walking shoes are essential. We recommend bringing water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), insect repellent, and a light snack. If hiking in summer, consider swimwear for a dip in the lake. Binoculars can enhance wildlife viewing.
